title: Investigation: Israel Still Targeting Journalists in Gaza During Ceasefire
detail: Prism Reports published an investigative piece on March 30, 2026 documenting that Israeli forces continued to target journalists in Gaza even after the ceasefire that took effect October 10, 2025. Time magazine simultaneously published a feature on journalists killed since the start of the Iran-Israel war. The CPJ's running count confirmed Israel had killed more journalists than any government since CPJ began tracking in 1992 — at least 259 confirmed journalist deaths in the Gaza conflict. The data included Palestinian journalists killed in Gaza, Lebanese journalists killed in the Lebanon front, and international correspondents. UNESCO condemned the pattern as a systematic attack on press freedom.

media.caption: Investigation: Israel Still Targeting Journalists in Gaza During Ceasefire

title: Netanyahu Orders Crackdown on Settler Violence in West Bank
detail: On March 29, 2026, Euronews published an exclusive report that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had issued a non-public directive ordering the IDF and police to crack down on settler violence ('nationalist crimes in Judea and Samaria'). The Israeli security cabinet had also officially regulated 30 previously illegal outposts in the West Bank. OCHA's March 27 report documented that 150+ settler attacks had been carried out since the regional escalation began, with 1,697 Palestinians already displaced in 2026 — already exceeding all of 2025. Bloomberg on March 28 published a feature showing West Bank farmers bearing the economic cost of unchecked settler violence. Critics noted Netanyahu's directive was non-public and lacked enforcement mechanisms.

media.caption: Netanyahu Orders Crackdown on Settler Violence in West Bank
